response.justification: Sarah Smith's background is almost entirely in web and application development (TypeScript, Node.js, D, JavaScript), with no meaningful C++ experience — the role's single most heavily-weighted minimum qualification is 8 years of C++ programming. Her only documented C++ work is a 6-month stint at National Instruments and a bachelor's thesis, totaling well under one year. She also lacks the required 5 years of large-scale infrastructure and distributed systems experience at the relevant depth, and has held no formal technical leadership role for cloud or systems engineering. While she demonstrates strong software craftsmanship habits and a genuine teaching instinct, her profile is a fundamental mismatch for a C++ cloud compute platform tech lead position.
strengths: Consistent practice of TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD, and pairing across multiple roles demonstrates strong software craftsmanship culture
High-throughput systems exposure at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby — low-latency microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O and high-performance databases
Active open-source contributor, workshop organizer, and conference speaker, showing technical communication skills relevant to a mentoring and alignment-focused leadership role
concerns: C++ experience is effectively absent — approximately 6 months at National Instruments plus a bachelor's thesis, far short of the mandatory 8-year minimum
No formal technical leadership or staff-level mentoring role focused on cloud or systems engineering; community education does not substitute for team-level technical leadership
Cloud architecture experience is limited to AWS CDK usage at Optilyz; no evidence of production ownership or design of a cloud compute platform at scale
